title: Haplophyllum albanicum
intro: Haplophyllum albanicum is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the Albanian myrtle or Albanian spurge. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Haplophyllum albanicum include limonoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Haplophyllum albanicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus or rue family. The plant is classified under the genus Haplophyllum and species albanicum. It is a perennial shrub native to Albania and is often found in mountainous regions. The classification of Haplophyllum albanicum reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rutaceae family. The plant's classification also helps in understanding its relationships with other species within the same genus and family.
uses: Haplophyllum albanicum is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Balkan region. Its medicinal uses include treating various ailments such as digestive issues and skin conditions due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating certain types of cancer and its antioxidant effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its therapeutic applications and potential side effects. Further studies on Haplophyllum albanicum's medicinal properties are necessary to confirm its effectiveness as a treatment for various health conditions.

It has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and gastritis, as well as respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. side_effects: Haplophyllum albanicum is a plant that may have potential side effects due to its toxic alkaloid content, including gastrointestinal issues and skin irritation upon contact.
